zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg於1501年出生在英國賀福爾盧沃迪時，她的父親Edward de Lugg38歲，母親Alice Shaw Lehman34歲。她於1523年與Henry Stephens在英國結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1552年3月16日在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾希爾莫頓享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾希爾莫頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg于1501年出生在英国賀福爾盧沃迪时，她的父亲Edward de Lugg38岁，母亲Alice Shaw Lehman34岁。她于1523年与Henry Stephens在英国结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1552年3月16日在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾希爾莫頓享年51岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾希爾莫頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lugg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): probably from a local vernacular derivative of Lucas . However, Reaney posits an Old English personal name,Lugga, from which this name could be derived.

variants.name: Lucas
meanings: English, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Lucas (Greek Loukas) ‘man from Lucania’. Lucania is a region of southern Italy thought to have been named in ancient times with a word meaning ‘bright’ or ‘shining’ (compare Lucio ). The Christian name owed its enormous popularity throughout Europe in the Middle Ages to Saint Luke the Evangelist, hence the development of this surname and many vernacular derivatives in most of the languages of Europe. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Loukas , Hungarian Lukács (see Lukacs ), German, Dutch, etc. Lukas , Polish Łukasz, Czech and Slovak Lukáš, Czech Lukeš, Slovenian and Croatian Lukež (see Lukes ). Compare Luke .
Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lùcais (see McLucas ).
History: Jacques Lucas dit Lépine from Port-en-Bessin-Huppain in Calvados, France, married Françoise Capel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1653.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Alexandra
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of   Alexander .  It was very little used in the English-speaking world before the 20th century, when it was brought in from Scandinavia and Eastern Europe. It owes its sudden rise in popularity in Britain at the end of the 19th century to  Queen Alexandra ,  Danish  wife of  Edward VII .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg was born in 1501, in Lugwardine, Herefordshire, England, her father, Edward de Lugg, was 38 and her mother, Alice Shaw Lehman, was 34. She married Henry Stephens in 1523, in England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 16 March 1552, in Hillmorton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in Hillmorton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Henry Stephens en 1523, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1552, à Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Alexandra de la Luggが1501年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアルグワーディンで生まれた時，父Edward de Luggは38歳，母Alice Shaw Lehmanは34歳であった。1523年にイギリスでHenry Stephensと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1552年3月16日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアヒルモートンで51歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアヒルモートンにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter's-Frocester, Gloucestershire, E: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 프록스터 스트 피터'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg(이)가 1501년 영국 허퍼드실르 루그워딘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward de Lugg(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Shaw Lehman(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1523년 영국에서 Henry Stephens(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1552년 3월 16일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 힐모르턴에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 힐모르턴에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1552, em Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Peter's-Frocester, Gloucestershire, E: ст питир'с чурч, фросестир, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg родилась в 1501 г. в местности лугвардаин, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edward de Lugg было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Shaw Lehman, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Stephens в 1523 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 16 марта 1552 г. в местности хиллмортон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности хиллмортон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.St Peter's-Frocester, Gloucestershire, E: St Peter's Church, Frocester, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Alice Alexandra de la Lugg nació en 1501, en Lugwardine, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward de Lugg, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Alice Shaw Lehman, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Henry Stephens en 1523, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1552, en Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Hillmorton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
